title: Bikkia neriifolia
intro: Bikkia neriifolia, also known as the Neriifolia Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The health benefits of Bikkia neriifolia include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in the treatment of various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bikkia neriifolia is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Bikkia neriifolia is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Bikkia neriifolia is a species within the genus Bikkia, which is native to the Pacific Islands and Australia. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest flowering plant families, with over 13,000 known species. The classification of Bikkia neriifolia reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants within the Rubiaceae family.
